items.title: Most of Tuvalu applied to relocate to Australia. But what happens if everyone leaves?

children.content: Tuvalu is building new land to hold back rising sea levels, but the majority of its people are applying to relocate to Australia. As they do, many are confronting a disturbing question.

gallery.altText: An aerial photo of land reclamation work in Tuvalu's capital Funafuti.
Falepili visa recipient Tevaogali Elisala with her four-year-old son Alfred on her knee.
A drone shot of Tuvalu's only international runway surrounded by ocean.

A drone shot of Tuvalu's only international runway surrounded by ocean.

items.title: Paul Keating backs CGT changes as 'structurally sound'

children.content: Former prime minister Paul Keating says the government's changes to capital gains tax are "structurally sound" and seek to "arrest" the growth in house prices. Follow live.

items.title: Labor burns political capital as tax reform backlash grows

items.title: Gen Zs who approach share market like 'lottery ticket' could pay more tax

children.content: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing in the stock market will likely pay more tax under the capital gains tax changes being proposed by the government.

items.title: 'Survival mode all the time': Australian parents break taboos on raising kids

children.content: As the birth rate hits a record low it's been revealed that almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national parenting survey.

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

items.title: Deciding to stay and rebuild after 'one-in-500-year' flood

children.content: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mis North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.

items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed. Here's why

items.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?

children.content: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.

image.altText: Green crops in the foreground with machinery in the background.

items.title: Outback landholders opening gate to large-scale renewable projects

children.content: If you mention renewable energy to agricultural producers in many regional communities, you will likely ruffle some feathers. But in far west NSW, landholders are showing preliminary support for a planned expansion of solar and wind energy assets.

image.altText: Infant boy sits on gate with his dad in outback arid location. Dad is wearing pink shirt and jeans.

items.title: Inside the 'worst managed infrastructure project in Australian history'

children.content: The Inland Rail has had funding slashed and the project placed on pause but Everald Compton has a bottle of Penfolds Grange waiting and even if it takes him till the age of 100, he's determined to see it through.

items.title: Prince William goes wild in crowd as Aston Villa ends 30-year drought

items.title: Recycling contractor accused of having employees sleep on floor sues for $5m
